ogTitle: Trungley Hall vs Trunkey Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Trungley Hall and Trunkey Creek.
heading: Trungley Hall vs Trunkey Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Trungley Hall, New South Wales and Trunkey Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Trungley Hall has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Trunkey Creek (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Trungley Hall or Trunkey Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Trungley Hall has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Trunkey Creek scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Trungley Hall scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Trunkey Creek scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Trungley Hall or Trunkey Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Trungley Hall has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
